content:
I literally said, I'm not gonna say this because I couldn't do it.

But you're not gonna say you're not gonna tell someone, oh, just look in the mirror and talk to yourself like that, because you're like, "What the fuck?

Like, that person literally went to school to learn how to write, like that, and they're not talking about themselves.

Damn.

And then I was like,, but right So then if you can make your little guy who you want the most confident, most badass yourself without any of your fears your ego or the mundane weighighing down on you, then, right, there's this ties into the next part, but I wonder if psychologically, if looking into the mirror, is that person's still telling yourself that's still, right, being the narrator of that story, but it's right, your brain can't perceive reality from imagination, and so I don't I think that not seeing yourself take some of it out of it, but I think you saying those words and your brain hearing it and reaffirming it is still gonna be extremely powerful.

Then on the flip side, how cool would it be to , not on the flip side, but in addition, like, right the fastest way to get where you're going is to admission it and meet your frequency there because if you can't imagine someone handing you $10,000 , how in the world is your nervous system ever going to correlate that actually coming in cheeri reality, right?
